$schema_note: Sprint brief for the Sanders Intelligence app. One file per micro-sprint (1 day cadence). Each task is a self-contained implementation prompt: outline, technical requirements, expected results, test coverage. Generated by the sprint-planner skill.
theme: Daily View foundation + task creation / interaction enhancements (per user direction)

project.name: Sanders intelligence app
project.stack: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite, Tailwind, React Router v6, TanStack Query v5, Supabase, Recharts

selection_rationale: Auto-task creation gating (1215103753564370) and the SI app DB connector (1214539638231269) closed since the last sprint, freeing capacity for the user-prioritized themes. Daily View is foundational and was the user's explicit #1 priority. The other 4 tasks were all added 27-28 May - max recency boost - and cluster around task-creation + task-view interaction, which the user named as #2 and #3 priorities. Tasks are ordered shortest -> longest so the day always lands something even if the Daily View slice runs long.
instructions_for_implementer: Work tasks in the 'order' field. For EACH task: read 'clarifying_questions' first and ask the user before writing code. Treat 'likely_files' as starting points for investigation, not the full surface. Implement against 'requirements', validate against 'completion_criteria', and write/extend tests per 'test_coverage'. When done, update the matching Asana task's completion status and leave a comment citing the file/line evidence (mirror the convention used when closing 1215004196463569).

added_mid_sprint: 1215143640254108 - BUG: Reconcile Excess Value across Executive Summary. Pulled in 2026-05-28 evening after the QA re-confirmed the bug. Highest-severity open issue; C-suite-facing data accuracy.
deferred_to_next_sprint: 1215103753564376 - Track no-impact completions / re-opens in Daily View (pushed to 2026-05-29; depends on Daily View structure landing first)

tasks.title: Tasks page - group by Assignee (4th GroupMode)
tasks.outline: Tasks page currently lets users group the list view by Status, Vendor, or Category. Add a fourth grouping: Assignee. Smallest win of the day and a clean structural extension of an existing pattern.
tasks.technical_description: TasksPage.tsx (line 27) defines `type GroupMode = 'status' | 'vendor' | 'category'`. The vendor grouping (lines ~203-218) uses an extractVendor() helper that reads from description; the assignee grouping is simpler because every task already has a typed `assignee: { name, email } | null` field populated by useTasks() (see hooks/useTasks.ts line 11). Add 'assignee' to the GroupMode union, render a fourth toggle button in the toolbar, and extend ListView() with an assignee-grouped branch that bins tasks by assignee.name with an 'Unassigned' fallback.
likely_files: app/src/pages/tasks/TasksPage.tsx - GroupMode union, the toolbar toggle buttons, ListView() grouping branches
app/src/hooks/useTasks.ts - assignee shape already available (no change expected)
app/src/types/index.ts - Task interface assignee field
requirements: Extend GroupMode union to include 'assignee'.
Add a fourth toggle button to the toolbar next to Status / Vendor / Category, with a sensible icon (e.g., User from lucide-react).
Add an assignee branch in ListView(): group filtered tasks by `task.assignee?.name`; tasks with no assignee go under 'Unassigned'.
Sort assignee groups alphabetically; 'Unassigned' always renders last.
Preserve the existing department filter behavior when assignee grouping is active.
completion_criteria: User can click an 'Assignee' button in the toolbar and the list re-groups by assignee.
An 'Unassigned' bucket appears at the bottom containing any tasks where assignee is null.
Department filter continues to work in assignee mode.
No console errors, no TypeScript errors, existing TasksPage behavior unchanged in other GroupModes.
expected_outcomes: Purchasing leads can quickly see what's on each team member's plate in one glance, especially useful as auto-task creation (just shipped) and auto-assignment (also this sprint) start to populate the queue.

unit: If TasksPage helpers are extracted (recommend doing so as part of this sprint): add `app/src/pages/tasks/TasksPage.helpers.ts` with groupByAssignee() and cover the Unassigned-last + alphabetical-otherwise sort with a fixture set.
integration: RTL render TasksPage with a fixture of 3 tasks (2 assigned to different users, 1 unassigned). Click the Assignee toggle and assert: two named groups exist, an 'Unassigned' group exists, 'Unassigned' renders last.
manual_qa: Toggle through all 4 GroupModes - confirm the right grouping renders each time.
Switch from Assignee mode to Vendor mode and back - confirm no state leak.

clarifying_questions: Should 'Unassigned' show only when there are unassigned tasks (hide when empty), or always render the bucket so it's discoverable?
Should the Board view also gain an assignee mode, or is it intentional that Board is always status?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

tasks.title: Auto-assign system-created tasks to the purchasing head
tasks.outline: Now that auto-task creation (1215103753564370) is live and emitting tasks with source='auto', the next gap is ownership. Currently auto-tasks land unassigned. Default them to Mordechai (or whoever the user designates as purchasing head) so the assignee column is never blank for system-generated work.
tasks.technical_description: The newly-landed autoTaskRules pipeline (added in the prior sprint - check app/src/lib/autoTaskRules.ts and the upload-complete wiring in UploadsPage.tsx) currently creates tasks without setting `assignee`. The Users table has Mordechai Rub (gid likely visible in users table; resolve at runtime via useUsers + role='purchasing' + department='purchasing'). Add a config: either (a) a constant DEFAULT_AUTO_ASSIGNEE_EMAIL in autoTaskRules.ts, or (b) a 'is_default_auto_assignee' boolean column on public.users that the rules query for. Prefer (b) so the assignee can be changed without a code deploy.
likely_files: app/src/lib/autoTaskRules.ts - where TaskDraft is built; add assigned_to to the draft
app/src/hooks/useTasks.ts - useCreateTask already accepts assigned_to; no change expected
app/src/pages/admin/UsersPage.tsx - add a 'Default auto-assignee' toggle/badge per user (if going with option b)
supabase/migrations/006_default_auto_assignee.sql - NEW migration adding the column (if going with option b)
requirements: All tasks emitted by autoTaskRules.runRules now include assigned_to set to the configured default-auto-assignee user's id.
If no default-auto-assignee is configured, fall back to the first admin user (so tasks never land truly unassigned).
If option (b) chosen: add migration with a partial unique index ensuring at most one user is is_default_auto_assignee=true; add a toggle to UsersPage that sets/unsets it.
If option (a) chosen: document the constant clearly in autoTaskRules.ts with a comment pointing to this Asana task.
completion_criteria: Trigger an auto-task generation (re-upload a CSV that satisfies the gating rule). The resulting task has assigned_to set to the designated user, visible on the Tasks page.
Sign in as the assigned user and confirm the task appears in their department filter view.
If config option (b): toggling the flag on a different user in UsersPage and re-triggering creates the next batch under the new user.
No regressions to manually-created tasks (they remain unassigned unless the user picks an assignee in TaskModal).
expected_outcomes: Auto-created tasks have a clear owner from the moment they land. Combined with the new Assignee grouping (task #1), the purchasing head can immediately see what the system has surfaced for them.

unit: Extend app/src/__tests__/autoTaskRules.test.ts: when default-auto-assignee is set, every TaskDraft has assigned_to equal to that user's id; when not set, falls back to first admin.
integration: If migration added: assert the partial unique index prevents two true values via a manual SQL test or a Supabase fixture.
manual_qa: Re-upload the fixture CSV from the auto-task creation sprint; confirm assignee is populated on the resulting task.

clarifying_questions: Confirm WHO the default auto-assignee is - Mordechai Rub specifically, or a generic 'purchasing head' role we should encode by department + role flag?
Option (a) hardcoded constant vs option (b) DB-driven toggle - which do you prefer? (b) is more flexible, (a) is faster to ship.
Should ALL auto-tasks go to one assignee, or eventually be routed by rule (price-review -> purchasing head; vendor-review -> vendor manager)?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files. The assignee identity is required before any code change.

tasks.title: Task comments - completion / postpone notes
tasks.outline: Tasks currently have no way to capture what happened when they were resolved. Add a comment thread so users can record completion notes ('ordered 200 cases'), postpone justifications ('vendor on holiday until Monday'), or back-and-forth context. Lays the groundwork for the postpone feature (task #4) which will write into the same thread.
tasks.technical_description: No comments table exists today (see the DB tables list in CLAUDE.md). Create a new public.task_comments table: id, task_id (FK), author_id (FK users), body, created_at, kind ('comment' | 'completion' | 'postpone'). On the client, extend TaskCard / TaskModal with a comments section: render existing comments inline (collapsed by default; click to expand), and a textarea + 'Add note' button. When a user advances a task to 'done' or 'cancelled' (or postpones - see task #4), prompt for a note that is saved as a comment with the appropriate kind.
likely_files: supabase/migrations/007_task_comments.sql - NEW: table + RLS (read for any auth, write for own or admin)
app/src/types/index.ts - TaskComment interface
app/src/hooks/useTaskComments.ts - NEW: useTaskComments(taskId), useAddTaskComment()
app/src/components/tasks/TaskCard - render comment count and expand-on-click
app/src/components/tasks/TaskModal.tsx - full comment thread in the edit view
app/src/components/tasks/CompletionPromptModal.tsx - NEW: small modal shown when status advances to done/cancelled
requirements: Migration creates task_comments with RLS: SELECT for any authenticated; INSERT for self only; UPDATE/DELETE for author or admin.
TaskCard shows a small comment count badge if comments exist; clicking it opens the TaskModal scrolled to the comments section.
TaskModal renders a chronological thread (oldest first) with author name, timestamp, and kind tag (Comment / Completion / Postponed).
TaskModal includes a textarea + 'Add note' button that posts a new comment with kind='comment'.

When user advances status to 'done' (CheckCircle button in TaskCard or save in TaskModal), show CompletionPromptModal with an optional 'How did this go?' textarea. Saving with text creates a comment with kind='completion'.
Empty textarea on completion = no comment created (don't force users to leave a note).
completion_criteria: A task can have N comments. The thread persists across page reloads.
Marking a task done with text in the prompt creates a comment with kind='completion'; marking done with empty text creates no comment.
Non-admin users cannot edit or delete others' comments (RLS).
Existing tasks without comments render identically to before (no UI noise).
No console errors, no TypeScript errors, existing TaskModal tests still pass.
expected_outcomes: Audit trail: every closed task can be inspected for 'how' and 'why'. Sets up the postpone-with-reason flow in task #4 and the 'tasks that had no impact on data' surface in 1215103753564376.

unit: app/src/__tests__/useTaskComments.test.ts: mock supabase to return a comment list and assert order + shape.
app/src/__tests__/CompletionPromptModal.test.tsx: empty textarea -> onSave called with null; filled textarea -> onSave called with the body string.
integration: RTL render TaskModal with a task fixture having 2 comments; assert both render in order with the right kind tag.
Add a comment via the form; assert useAddTaskComment fires with the expected payload.
manual_qa: Create a task, mark it done with a note, reload the page, confirm the comment persists with kind='completion'.
Sign in as a non-admin user, try to delete an admin's comment - should fail silently (RLS) or show an error toast.

clarifying_questions: Should comments support markdown / @mentions, or plain text only? (Plain is recommended for v1.)
Inline thread on the TaskCard (always visible) vs collapsed-by-default with a 'View N notes' link?
Should the completion prompt be modal-blocking or a toast with a quick-add? Modal forces a moment of reflection; toast keeps flow.
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files. The markdown/inline-vs-collapsed decisions materially shape the component design.

tasks.title: Postpone task action - cancel without re-triggering auto-create
tasks.outline: When a user decides a system-generated task is genuinely not actionable right now (vendor unreachable, waiting on info), they want to clear it from the active queue WITHOUT having auto-task creation re-emit the same task on the next CSV upload. 'Postpone' is the new state that says 'don't auto-recreate this for N days'.
tasks.technical_description: Adds a third terminal state alongside done and cancelled. Implementation: (a) add 'postponed' to TaskStatus, (b) add postponed_until DATE column to tasks, (c) add a Postpone button to TaskCard (next to Edit/Delete), (d) extend autoTaskRules dedupe to consider postponed-and-not-yet-expired tasks as still 'open' for dedupe purposes, (e) on postpone, prompt for a duration (3/7/14/30 days) and a note (saved as a task_comment with kind='postpone' from task #3).
likely_files: supabase/migrations/008_task_postpone.sql - NEW: add postponed_until column, allow 'postponed' value in status check constraint
app/src/types/index.ts - TaskStatus union
app/src/pages/tasks/TasksPage.tsx - STATUS_COLS map, NEXT_STATUS map, TaskCard postpone button
app/src/components/tasks/PostponeModal.tsx - NEW: duration + note picker
app/src/lib/autoTaskRules.ts - dedupe filter must skip rules whose dedupe-key matches an open OR postponed-until-future task
app/src/hooks/useTasks.ts - useUpdateTaskStatus needs to accept postponed_until
requirements: Add 'postponed' to TaskStatus type and to the DB check constraint.
Add postponed_until DATE nullable column on public.tasks.
TaskCard renders a 'Postpone' button (clock-with-arrow icon) for tasks in status 'todo' or 'in_progress'.

Clicking Postpone opens PostponeModal with duration radio (3/7/14/30 days) and a note textarea. Save sets status='postponed', postponed_until = today+duration, and writes a task_comment with kind='postpone'.
Postponed tasks are filtered out of the default Tasks list view (hidden behind a 'Show postponed (N)' toggle, mirroring the snoozed-rows pattern in ActionCenter).
autoTaskRules.runRules must NOT create a new task if a postponed task with the same auto-signature exists AND postponed_until > today.

When postponed_until passes, the task auto-reverts to 'todo' status (background job or on-read trigger - simplest: useTasks hook re-evaluates on fetch and writes status='todo' for any postponed_until <= today).
completion_criteria: Postpone a task for 7 days. Re-upload a CSV that would otherwise trigger the auto-rule for the same SKU/vendor - no new task is created.
Wait 8 days (or manually backdate postponed_until in the DB). On the next page load, the task reverts to 'todo' and is visible again.
Postpone creates a task_comment with kind='postpone' containing the user's note.
Toggle 'Show postponed' reveals the hidden tasks with a clear visual treatment.
Existing snooze / dismiss flow in ActionCenter is unaffected (this is task-level, that is action-level).
expected_outcomes: Purchasing can mute noisy auto-tasks for a defined window without losing the underlying signal - the task comes back when relevant. Reduces 'task fatigue' from the new auto-creation pipeline.

unit: Extend autoTaskRules.test.ts: dedupe filter excludes drafts that match a postponed-and-not-yet-expired existing task; INCLUDES drafts where the matching postponed task has postponed_until in the past.
Test the on-read revert logic: a task with status='postponed' and postponed_until in the past gets normalized to 'todo' before being returned by useTasks.
integration: RTL render TaskCard, click Postpone, fill the modal, save. Assert useUpdateTaskStatus is called with status='postponed' and the correct postponed_until.
manual_qa: End-to-end: upload, see auto-task, postpone for 3 days, re-upload, confirm no new task. Manually backdate postponed_until, refresh Tasks page, confirm the task is back as 'todo'.

clarifying_questions: Should postpone duration also offer a custom date picker (not just preset 3/7/14/30)?
When a postponed task auto-reverts to 'todo', should we add a system comment ('Auto-reactivated after 7-day postponement') for the audit trail?
Should postpone count toward the 're-opened' metric for 1215103753564376 (tracking tasks that had no impact)?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files. Depends on task #3 (task_comments table) - implement #3 first or merge their migrations.

tasks.title: My Daily View - MVP slice (tasks due today, completed yesterday, counters)
tasks.outline: First slice of the Daily View. New /daily route that gives the logged-in user a focused 'what's on my plate today' page, plus a glance at what was completed yesterday and simple counters (created today, completed today, due today). No calendar integration yet - that's a follow-up sprint. Foundation that 1215103753564376 (no-impact completions) and 1215004196463583 (Phase 2 DoD view) will both build on.
tasks.technical_description: New page at app/src/pages/work/DailyView.tsx, wired in App.tsx as /daily for all roles, with a nav link added under Work in Sidebar.tsx. Data comes from useTasks() (already returns assignee-scoped tasks for purchasing role) plus a new useTaskMetrics(date) hook that aggregates created/completed counts from public.tasks. The view has three sections: (1) 'Today' - tasks where assigned_to = me AND (due_date = today OR status = 'in_progress'), (2) 'Completed yesterday' - tasks where assigned_to = me AND status = 'done' AND updated_at::date = yesterday, (3) a strip of 3 counter cards: Created today / Completed today / Due today.
likely_files: app/src/pages/work/DailyView.tsx - NEW page
app/src/pages/work/DailyView.helpers.ts - NEW: partitionDailyTasks(tasks, today), computeCounters(tasks, today)
app/src/__tests__/DailyView.helpers.test.ts - NEW tests
app/src/hooks/useTaskMetrics.ts - NEW hook (or fold into useTasks if minimal)
app/src/App.tsx - new route /daily
app/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx - add 'My Day' link in the Work group
requirements: New route /daily accessible to all roles.
Page header shows the current date and the logged-in user's name.
Three counter cards at the top: 'Created today' (N), 'Completed today' (N), 'Due today' (N). Match the existing KPICard component style.
'Today' section lists tasks assigned to me, ordered by priority desc then due_date asc. Each row uses the existing TaskCard component (reuse, do not fork).
'Completed yesterday' section lists my tasks completed on yesterday's date, collapsed by default with a 'Show N' link.
Empty states for each section ('Nothing due today', 'Nothing completed yesterday').
Sidebar link 'My Day' in the Work group, above Tasks.
Page must be fast: do all aggregation client-side from the existing useTasks() result; no extra round-trip unless the metrics counters require it.
completion_criteria: Sign in as Andrew (admin), navigate to /daily - page loads with at least the empty-state version even if no tasks exist.
Create a task assigned to Andrew due today; it appears in the Today section and the 'Due today' counter increments.
Mark a task done; it disappears from Today, the 'Completed today' counter increments. If marked done yesterday (manually backdate updated_at), it appears under 'Completed yesterday'.
Counters reconcile with the lists: 'Due today' count = number of rows in Today section assigned to me with due_date = today.
Sidebar 'My Day' link is highlighted when on /daily.
expected_outcomes: Each user has a personal landing for 'what should I be doing today' - reduces the cognitive cost of opening Tasks page and filtering. Strategic foundation: 1215103753564376 will plug a fourth section ('Reopened - your completion didn't stick') into this page, and 1215004196463583 will add a DoD delta panel.

unit: app/src/__tests__/DailyView.helpers.test.ts: partitionDailyTasks correctly bucketizes a fixture of 10 tasks (mix of due dates, statuses, assignees) into today/yesterday/other.
computeCounters returns the right N for each of the 3 counters given a fixture.
integration: RTL render DailyView with a mocked useTasks() returning the fixture; assert the section headers, counters, and TaskCard count per section.
Empty state: render with no matching tasks; assert each section shows its empty message.
manual_qa: /daily as admin, /daily as purchasing - confirm both load and show role-appropriate data.
Click a TaskCard from /daily - it should open the same TaskModal as the Tasks page (no fork).
Toggle sidebar collapsed/expanded - 'My Day' link still works.

clarifying_questions: What's the right route - /daily, /my-day, or /tasks/today? (skill convention has used /tasks for the page; /daily is short and discoverable.)
Should 'Today' include tasks assigned to me that are OVERDUE (due_date < today, status not done)? Recommend yes, with an overdue badge.
Should this page eventually be the landing page for non-admin users (HomeRedirect change), or always opt-in via the sidebar?
Calendar integration is explicitly out of scope - confirm we're shipping without it this sprint?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files. The route name and overdue-inclusion decisions affect every downstream task that builds on this view.

tasks.title: BUG: Reconcile Excess Value across Executive Summary (single definition in financialMetrics)
tasks.added_reason: Pulled into today's scope after the 2026-05-28 QA confirmed the bug is still present (filed 2026-05-26). Highest-severity open issue: it shows wrong numbers on the C-suite landing page where capital-allocation decisions are made.
tasks.outline: Executive Summary shows two different 'Excess' dollar values within ~6 inches of each other: the Inventory Health bar reports $22.65M, the Excess Value KPI card reports $14.98M. Both are computed correctly in isolation but represent different metrics (full on-hand cost of excess-flagged stock vs. the over-threshold portion from the CSV). They share the same label, so an executive sees one number as 'wrong'. Fix: pick ONE canonical definition, route every consumer through a single helper in lib/financialMetrics.ts, add a test pinning the contract.
tasks.technical_description:
The Executive Summary page has FIVE places that compute 'excess value', using TWO different definitions:

Definition A - sum of `on_hand_value` for rows with status in ('Excess stock','Surplus orders'). Value today: $22,651,752.
  1) ExecutiveSummary.tsx:35 - feeds statusValueBreakdown[1].value, which drives the Inventory Health bar (line 131,151) AND the Inventory Value Distribution pie chart (line 206).
  2) ExecutiveSummary.helpers.ts:100 - buildTopRiskSuppliers() per-supplier excessValue field used in the 'Top Risk Supplier' list.

Definition B - sum of `excess_value` CSV column for rows with the same status filter. Value today: $14,984,676.
  3) useInventory.ts:83 - feeds kpis.excessValue, which drives the Excess Value KPI card (ExecutiveSummary.tsx:183).
  4) ExecutiveSummary.tsx:64 - per-brand excessValue feeding the 'Excess Value by Brand (Top 8)' bar chart.
  5) Implicit: anywhere downstream that reads kpis.excessValue.

The KPI card subtitle reads 'tied up in overstock' - that phrasing describes Definition A (working capital locked up in overstocked SKUs), not Definition B (over-threshold liquidation potential). Strong tell that A is the intended canonical and B is the drift.

Fix: introduce `sumExcessValue(records)` in app/src/lib/financialMetrics.ts using Definition A. Route all five call sites through it. Optionally expose Definition B as a separately labelled 'Overstock Above Threshold' metric in a follow-up, but do NOT ship two excess metrics in the same sprint.
likely_files: app/src/lib/financialMetrics.ts - NEW: export sumExcessValue(records: InventoryRecord[]): number using Definition A. Co-locate with the existing deriveFinancialPercentages helper.

app/src/hooks/useInventory.ts:83 - replace `excessValue += record.excess_value` with the helper (call once after the loop, or accumulate on_hand_value inline). Same change at line ~162 (the second pagination path).
app/src/pages/csuite/ExecutiveSummary.tsx:35 - replace inline filter+reduce with sumExcessValue(records).

app/src/pages/csuite/ExecutiveSummary.tsx:64 - per-brand excess. Change `r.excess_value` to `r.on_hand_value` so the brand bar chart agrees with the rest of the page. (The chart title may want a clarifying subtitle, e.g. 'On-hand value of overstocked SKUs'.)
app/src/pages/csuite/ExecutiveSummary.helpers.ts:100 - already uses on_hand_value; refactor to call sumExcessValue for consistency.
app/src/__tests__/financialMetrics.test.ts - NEW (also closes 1215143337734271).

app/src/__tests__/ExecutiveSummary.helpers.test.ts - add a cross-consistency test: the health-bar value, the KPI card value, the pie chart Excess slice, and the brand bar chart sum must all match within $0.01 for the same fixture.
CLAUDE.md - document the canonical definition under a new 'Inventory Status Values' subsection so future contributors don't re-introduce the divergence.
requirements: Introduce sumExcessValue(records) in lib/financialMetrics.ts using Definition A (on_hand_value of rows where status is 'Excess stock' OR 'Surplus orders'). Pure function, no side effects.
Replace all five call sites (listed in likely_files) with the helper or its equivalent inline pattern (record.on_hand_value).

After the change, reload /executive and confirm: Inventory Health bar Excess === Excess Value KPI card === sum of Excess Value by Brand bar chart === Inventory Value Distribution pie chart Excess slice. All four must agree to the cent.

Per-supplier excessValue in 'Top Risk Supplier' rows must also reconcile: sum of all per-supplier excessValue (when no filter) === all-vendor total from the helper. (Already true today since helpers.ts uses Definition A; just guard with a test.)
No new column or new label introduced. Keep the existing 'Excess Value' label and 'tied up in overstock' subtitle on the KPI card - they're correct under Definition A.
If the over-threshold CSV column (excess_value) is still useful elsewhere, leave it untouched in the data model - we're only changing what the Excess Value aggregate means in the UI.
Migration: NONE required. This is a UI-layer fix; the CSV columns are unchanged.
completion_criteria: On /executive with the current upload: all four 'Excess' surfaces show the same dollar value (currently ~$22.65M under Definition A).

On /executive with a fixture upload where excess_value column !== on_hand_value (e.g., excess_value zero but status is Excess stock - intentional bad data): all four surfaces still agree, all showing the on_hand_value-based number.
Top Risk Supplier list per-supplier excessValue rows sum to the all-vendor total within $1.00 (allow tiny rounding).
financialMetrics.test.ts asserts sumExcessValue is the sum of on_hand_value for rows with the two excess statuses, ignores other statuses, returns 0 on empty input, and returns 0 when no rows match.
ExecutiveSummary.helpers.test.ts asserts the cross-consistency contract: same input -> same output across helpers, pie data, and KPI snapshot.
cd app && npm test passes; no TypeScript errors; no console errors on /executive load.
BUG 1215143640254108 closed in Asana with a comment citing the new helper file:line and the five replaced call sites.
expected_outcomes: Executives see ONE consistent 'Excess' number across the page. No more 'is it $15M or $23M?' confusion. The number reads correctly under the existing 'tied up in overstock' framing - working capital locked up in overstocked SKUs.

The codebase grows a single source of truth for excess-value aggregation, matching the pattern already established by deriveFinancialPercentages for COGS%/Margin%. Future feature additions cannot easily re-introduce the drift.
Closes a high-priority C-suite-facing data accuracy bug that has been open for 2 days.

unit: - sumExcessValue with mixed statuses returns the sum of on_hand_value for Excess stock + Surplus orders rows only
- sumExcessValue ignores rows where on_hand_value is null/undefined (treat as 0)
- sumExcessValue returns the same number when the same set of rows is passed twice (purity)
integration: - Given a 12-row fixture spanning all 6 statuses, the health-bar Excess slice, the KPI excessValue, the pie chart Excess slice, and the sum of brand bar values all equal sumExcessValue(fixture).
- Given a fixture where excess_value CSV column is intentionally divergent from on_hand_value, the aggregate STILL uses on_hand_value (regression guard against re-introducing Definition B).
manual_qa: Reload /executive as admin. Read the four Excess numbers (health bar legend, KPI card, pie chart tooltip on the Excess segment, brand bar chart sum). Confirm all four match to the cent.
Apply a category filter via the URL (e.g., /executive doesn't have one yet, so navigate to /purchasing/inventory?status=Excess+stock and verify the row count + total matches the health bar value).
Click the Excess segment of the health bar - the drill-through to /purchasing/inventory?status=Excess+stock should land on a filtered list whose visible total matches the bar's value.

clarifying_questions: Confirm Definition A (on_hand_value of excess-status rows) is the intended canonical. Recommendation: YES - matches the KPI card subtitle 'tied up in overstock' and matches what 3 of 5 current call sites already use. If you prefer Definition B (the CSV column), the work flips direction but is the same size.

Should we add a second metric 'Overstock Above Threshold' (Definition B, the CSV column) somewhere on the page in a follow-up? Recommendation: NOT this sprint - ship the fix clean, evaluate need later.
Should the brand bar chart subtitle clarify the metric ('On-hand value of overstocked SKUs by brand')? Recommendation: yes, one-line subtitle to prevent future regression.
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files. The Definition A vs B call materially changes what every downstream view shows; do not start coding until confirmed.
